scope.summary: Close out P0.1 contract drift and record that P0.2-P0.3 in sec.25.2 are satisfied by the current quality graph.
in_scope: quality required-caller workflows (required.yml, canary.yml, soak.yml, release-qualification.yml)
out_of_scope: Other CI optimization work outside the sec.25.2 P0.1-P0.3 acceptance checks
Unrelated release or runtime stability risks outside quality-gate wiring

entry_points.name: P0.1 _quality split retained across all caller profiles
entry_points.trace: rg scans show required.yml/canary.yml/soak.yml/release-qualification.yml each call ./.github/workflows/_quality.yml; git history shows this wiring is in 72dfa61, and _quality still uses shard matrix fail-fast=false + fuzz shard 0..3.

entry_points.name: P0.2 fuzz fanout + ASan cache + fuzz workspace cache
entry_points.trace: In _quality.yml, fuzz job uses strategy matrix shard: [0, 1, 2, 3] (fail-fast:false), workspaces include `. -> target` and `crates/oracledb-protocol/fuzz -> target`, and fuzz is invoked via `cargo fuzz build --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`; current commit 4f1eb3a in file history owns these entries.

entry_points.name: P0.3 suite duplication check
entry_points.trace: rg for `cargo test --workspace --exclude oracledb-pyshim` returns exactly one strict workspace test command (`quality-test-workspace`) and one docs/doc-test command (`--doc`), with no duplicate strict workspace test in other shards.

entry_points.name: P0.1 contract fix
entry_points.trace: New commit 46ec421 adds `id: free_disk_space_for_the_test` and records its outcome, then updates `EXPECTED_QUALITY_COMMANDS` with `Free disk space for the test` in verify_required_local.py.

limitations: I did not rerun full `scripts/verify_required_local.py --required` against a live runner here; I verified graph-contract and self-tests plus targeted command audits in-tree.
